notes: Can't get it to run. First there is an error screen saying the VRAM was recognised as 0.75 GB (real size: 2 GB). Then the start screen does not recognise my 360 controller, even in Big Picture with the right config. After telling wine explicitly that the GPU has 2 GB (with protontricks), the error message says 1 GB recognised. Either way, not going to play this with keyboard and mouse. Also, why is there no exit button on the start screen? Do you really need to Alt-Tab out and close the task? Does not seem right. So, to state it clearly, I have not gone into the game, because of no controller. The VRAM recognised as 1 GB may or may not lead to issues later in the game. I would not know. Will be waiting for a fix for this.

title: No Man's Sky

notes:
!! THIS REVIEW IS ONLY FOR THE EXPERIMENTAL BRANCH !!
The stable branch works perfectly fine as many reviews describe it.

Hello Games just released an update where they switched from OpenGL to Vulkan (see https://www.nomanssky.com/2019/04/vulkan-update/).
This update is only available on the experimental branch for now (the stable branch will switch to Vulkan some time in the future).

When opening the game a popup appears saying that Vulkan is not supported and you should update your graphics drivers.
After dismissing the error the game starts anyway.
The Menu and loading screen work fine, but the actual game runs with 5-6 fps.
title: No Man's Sky

notes: Didn't work with OpenGL but vulkan runs flawless. Beta password for vulkan demo is `3xperimental`
title: No Man's Sky

notes: Nothing to note. it runs better than on windows with the same settings.
title: No Man's Sky

notes:
[experimental branch] works with latest AMDVLK (v-2019.Q2.1) (Vulkan Header: 1.1.105). Got a stable 60 fps and prolly more, but am on a headless setup atm, limited by in-home streaming. I suspect tho, 90 fps is possible with everything on Ultra.
Had to disable VSync or the game would not launch.
There are build instructions for AMDVLK on github and it can be launched by referencing the VK_ICD_FILENAMES="path_to amd_icd64.json" in steam command options or Lutris. For No mans sky you just can select Radeon FX in the in game graphics menu after you built AMDVLK, as a second graphics driver, no referencing needed.

On RADV I dont get more than 15 fps (Vulkn Header 1.1.70)

title: No Man's Sky

notes: After the Beyond update, NMS crashed on loading a previous save. I was able to create a new save and turn down the graphics options to the Enhanced preset, which also caused a crash but allowed me to load my old save again after relaunching. Performance is horrible compared to pre-Beyond, ~30fps with minimum settings and 15-20fps with Enhanced. Any higher presets are unplayable and may immediately crash the game. Before Beyond (but also with older Proton versions so I don't know which thing caused the performance difference), I usually got ~60fps with relatively high settings. Psensor shows low to no CPU or VRAM usage while playing, but GPU usage maxed out (pre-Beyond, it would show very CPU and mid-to-high GPU and VRAM usage). I have not yet tried any tweaks other than the settings within the game.

title: No Man's Sky

notes: I'm posting a new report now that the "Beyond" update has launched.  Previously I was able to play with very good performance using the OpenGL renderer.  Unfortunately, the new update has completely removed the OpenGL renderer in favor of Vulkan.  This would ordinarily be a good thing, because Vulkan performs better, but there appears to be a critical issue with the Nvidia Linux driver + NMS Vulkan renderer than causes extremely low performance.  Please post here if you would like Nvidia to fix the issue: https://devtalk.nvidia.com/default/topic/1050274/linux/no-mans-sky-proton-vulkan-patch-performance-issues-with-nvidia/
title: No Man's Sky

notes:
Seems to run better than Windows, on higher settings too.
One recommended fix I've personally found myself is to edit the file: steamapps/common/No Man's Sky/Binaries/SETTINGS/TKGRAPHICSSETTINGS.MXML and set "TerrainTessellation" to Low.

Without this change, I would crash to desktop on the white screen "Initialize"

title: No Man's Sky

notes:
Game runs well enough, get a decent 30fps running around. I'm sure I'll experience more issues later on but for now it all works.

When I firsted started the game up it crashed pretty much just before the white "initialize" screen. But after changing proton version, Setting D3, disabling fsync and a bunch of other random tweaks it kept crashing.
Turns out the problem was that it didn't like anything else running on my machine, even though only 6GB RAM was being used and only 5% CPU usage, it only stopped crashing when I closed EVERYTHING on my computer before launching the game.

After launching game with EVERYTHING closed, game runs without a hitch at a stable 30 FPS spiking up to 60. NO tweaks enabled. Just base game with Proton 4.11-2.
title: No Man's Sky

notes: The game crashed every-time after the load screen until I changed "TerrainTessellation" to Low in the TKGRAPHICSSETTINGS.MXML file. ~60FPS but I have no sound.
title: No Man's Sky

notes: It runs without problem at the first try. I set the graphics to the minimum after having fps drops. After that fps are still very unstable but in general it is playable. Though the fps depends on the weather & plantet you are on. Flying over a planet is also very laggy...
title: No Man's Sky

notes:
I was going to rate this game Platinum as it used to work perfectly out of the box pre-beyond (version 2.0), but I thought I'd wait for VR and include that in my review. 

Unfortunately, when Beyond arrived, it made the game unplayable. From what I've read here though, it would seem that this is a Vulkan driver issue and is probably only going to be temporary.

As it ran in ultra-slow motion while loading and then crashed before I could get into the game itself, I was not able to change the settings in game, but I was able to edit them via ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/No Man's Sky/Binaries/SETTINGS/TKGRAPHICSSETTINGS.MXML

I replaced all entries of High with Low, set MaxframeRate to 30, set VsyncEx to Off, set fullscreen to false and borderless to True.

Pancake version works fine again now. 

As for VR, trying to load the VR version plays the music in the headset but opens the game on the monitor. VR view just shows the loading screen. I have the Valve Index.

I'll probably end up experimenting with turning various settings back up, and will keep trying with VR on every update and will report back if anything improves.

TL;DR: Platinum before Beyond, Silver now (for now)
title: No Man's Sky

notes:
On mesa-aco compiler it runs especially well, no stuttering and 120 fps on Ultra settings. Same goes for the standard llvm compiler, just a bit more initial stuttering. Both run on RADV tho and are missing textures as well are causing a gpu crash after some time of playing or just from the start.

AMDVLK 2019 Q3_4 Vulkan Loader runs perfect. Graphics are perfect and fps is around 120 - 160. Yet it is not possible to start the game with amdPalSettings.cfg set in place to produce a ShaderCache to disc. So here is heavy stuttering everytime anew when you launch the game.

title: No Man's Sky

notes:
In order to be able to start the game, the value in the game settings for "TerrainTesselation" must be set to "Low". 

The setting must be made in the file TKGRAPHICSSETTINGS.MXML. This is located in the folder ~/steam/steamapps/common/No Man's Sky/Binaries/SETTINGS/.

If you open the file with an editor you will find the following line:
<Property name="TerrainTessellation" value="TkGraphicsDetailTypes.xml">

The next line after that is:
<Property name="GraphicDetail" value="High" />

In this line replace the "High" with a "Low". After that I could at least load my existing save games again. Before I had a crash to desktop after loading the game in the moment where the game starts.
title: No Man's Sky

notes: Did not run until I updated my kernel to the latest version and installed beta drivers. Performance was terrible using stock proton, but almost anything else works (wine, tkg, ge)

title: No Man's Sky

notes:
After the recent "Beyond update" this game is completely broke and unstable without tweaks for Nvidia users. First you should modify your TKGRAPHICSSETTINGS.MXML like everyone suggested before by settings the "TerrainTessellation" value to "Low".

Now the crucial part is compiling a hacky work around to spoof your vendorid to be AMD rather than Nvidia. Clone this repo ( https://github.com/volca02/spoof_vendorid ) and compile it. In steam, edit the "Set launch options" for No Man Sky with the value shown on the git repo. Additional info can be found here ( https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/438#issuecomment-522725441 )

Once this is done, I was able to play No Man Sky again perfectly with no crashing or FPS issues for over 10 hours, just like it was before the Beyond update!

title: No Man's Sky

notes:
Game runs smoother than it did in the previous major update, even in menus. It does require turning off terrain tessellation via editing TKGRAPHICSSETTINGS.MXML in a text editor to prevent a crash when loading into a save on AMD. To do this, find the "TerrainTessellation" property, remove the trailing slash, add another Property tag below it with name="GraphicDetail" and value="Low". Then close both property tags and save the file. No other tweaks were necessary for me.
Oh, and as of the time of this writing, keyboard controls can't be remapped, but that's a bug in the game, not a Proton issue.
title: No Man's Sky

I can't comment on the stability of the game either because I played for 5 minutes and gave up due to the performance.

notes:
As mentioned in previous, if you find you are crashing once the world has loaded, you may need to set 'TerrainTessellation" to low in the TKGRAPHICSSETTINGS.MXML file, found at : 

~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/No Man's Sky/Binaries/SETTINGS. Once changed, it runs great, a few weird behaviours on a couple of textures, but its certainly the best I've seen the game run so far. 

For users on Nvidia, see the guides below, as the current version requires you spoof your Vendor ID to replicate AMD. News on the grapevine is Hello Games have recognised the issue.

notes:
Fix crash when loading shaders on Beyond!
DELETE vulkan-1.dll
https://ibb.co/gzXBvNg
title: No Man's Sky

notes:
Fix #2 crash when loading shaders to Beyond!
1. Disable in Steam: shader cache
2. Remove Steam/steamapps/shadercache
3. Remove Steam/steamapps/common/No Man's Sky/GAMEDATA/SHADERCACHE
4. Remove all .MXML Steam/steamapps/common/No Man's Sky/Binaries/SETTINGS
5. Restart "no man's sky" at least 5 times after each reboot!!!
title: No Man's Sky

notes:
At first start game would crash right after character/save has been loaded. 
So like many have suggested,I have opened file: TKGRAPHICSSETTINGS.MXML file, found at : ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/No Man's Sky/Binaries/SETTINGS then found following option "TerrainTessellation" and for that option I have set "GraphicDetail" value to be equal to "Low".

After this tweak, game did not crash occasional stuttering happens and sometimes textures on Frigates do not load, specifically place between hangar and bridge. I cannot remember exactly what fixed it(happens rarely though) recalling the frigate or just going into another system will work.

Additional note: MODS work without any problems, just gotta pay attention to file conflicts which is noted on mods description page usually.

title: No Man's Sky

notes:
Same as people have noted here: after loading a save game, game crashes.
What helped me:
1) Disable shader caching in "Steam Settings - Shader Pre-Caching"
2) Delete Steam/steamapps/shadercache
3) Delete Steam/steamapps/common/No Man's Sky/GAMEDATA/SHADERCACHE
4) Delete all .MXML Steam/steamapps/common/No Man's Sky/Binaries/SETTINGS
5) Start the game and load a save game
6) If still crashes open ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/No Man's Sky/Binaries/SETTINGS and set "GraphicDetail" under "TerrainTessellation" to "Low".
Had terrible fps for the first few days until I did a complete shut down for PC. This made the game playable the same as it was on Windows.

title: No Man's Sky

notes:
Contrary to other reports, I have the same problem as always:  the game ran at about 1/2 the FPS it does in Windows even in the experimental branch, until tweaks are done to spoof it into believing I run an AMD video card.

To recap (from earlier comments) - you need to set up spoof_vendorid (follow directions at https://github.com/volca02/spoof_vendorid/blob/master/README.md to set it up), and possibly (I don't know if this is needed but it's what I did) switch to the experimental branch of this game by right clicking on it in Steam -> Properties -> Beta -> enter the code "3xperimental" and check code, then switch and apply/download.

Works great after that, but these steps are still necessary for me while others say they no longer are required.

title: No Man's Sky

notes:
Since I am using bumblebee on my Arch install, I tried setting "optirun %command%" in the launch options but got an error about Vulkan not being installed. I found this package that allows using Vulkan via primus: https://github.com/felixdoerre/primus_vk

After installing that package and setting the launch options to "ENABLE_PRIMUS_LAYER=1 optirun %command%", the game runs perfectly at Ultra settings!
